css: @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Dancing+Script:wght@600;700&family=Outfit:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap'); #custom-post-people-pleasing { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #fff7f7 0%, #faecec 100%); width: 100%; border-radius: 1rem; box-shadow: 0 15px 45px rgba(0,0,0,0.05), 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.02); padding: 2.5rem 1.25rem; margin: 0 auto 2rem; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; overflow-x: hidden; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .pin-title { font-family: 'Outfit', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; font-size: inherit; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: normal; margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 2rem; width: 100%; max-width: 100%; text-align: center; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .description-container { margin-bottom: 2.5rem; text-align: center; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.05); padding-bottom: 1.5rem; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .description-text { font-size: 1.05rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.6; font-style: italic; max-width: 44rem; margin: 0 auto; text-align: left; padding: 0 0.5rem; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-list { width: 100%; margin: 0 auto; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-item { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 2.5rem; padding-bottom: 2.5rem; gap: 0.5rem; position: relative; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-item:not(:last-child)::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: 0; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); width: 80px; height: 2px; background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.06); border-radius: 2px; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-number { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 800; color: #b8b8b8; padding-top: 5px; min-width: 1.2rem; text-align: left; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .item-content { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; flex: 1; min-width: 0; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .affirmation-text { font-family: 'Dancing Script', cursive; font-size: 1.85rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1a1a1a; line-height: 1.3; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-text { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 400; color: #444; line-height: 1.7; border-left: 3px solid #cbd5e1; padding-left: 1rem; margin-top: 0.5rem; } @media (min-width: 768px) { #custom-post-people-pleasing { max-width: 52rem; border-radius: 1.5rem; padding: 4.5rem 3.5rem; margin: 3rem auto 5rem; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .pin-title { margin-bottom: 2.5rem; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .description-container { margin-bottom: 3.5rem; padding-bottom: 2rem; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-item { gap: 1rem; margin-bottom: 3.5rem; padding-bottom: 3.5rem; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-item:not(:last-child)::after { width: 120px; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-number { font-size: 1.4rem; min-width: 1.8rem; text-align: right; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .affirmation-text { font-size: 2.4rem; } #custom-post-people-pleasing .prompt-text { font-size: 1.25rem; line-height: 1.8; padding-left: 1.5rem; } }
html:
10 Affirmations & Journal Prompts to Stop People-Pleasing & Own Your Truth

Breaking the cycle of people-pleasing starts with recognizing your own value. Use these affirmations and journal prompts to reclaim your voice, set healthy boundaries, and start living authentically for yourself.

1.
My worth is not tied to how much I do for others.
When was the last time you said 'yes' to something you wanted to say 'no' to? What fear drove that decision, and what would have happened if you said no?
2.
I am allowed to disappoint others to protect my own peace.
Whose disappointment are you most afraid of right now? Write down why your own well-being is more important than their temporary discomfort.
3.
My needs, opinions, and feelings are valid and matter.
Think of a situation where you stayed quiet to keep the peace. If you had spoken your true thoughts, what would you have said?
4.
I do not have to earn love or respect through endless accommodation.
In what relationships do you feel like you have to 'work' to be loved? How can you start showing up as just yourself in those spaces?
5.
Setting boundaries is an act of self-care, not selfishness.
What is one boundary you desperately need to set this week? Write down the exact words you will use to communicate it.
6.
I release the need to be the 'fixer' in everyone else's life.
Who are you currently trying to 'save' or 'fix'? What would happen if you stepped back and let them handle their own problems?
7.
It is safe for me to be disliked by people who misunderstand me.
How much time do you spend worrying about what others think of you? If you stopped caring today, what would you do differently?
8.
I trust my inner voice over the expectations of the world.
What is a dream or desire you have hidden because you thought others would judge it? Write about it freely without self-censorship.
9.
I forgive myself for abandoning my own needs in the past.
Write a short letter of apology to yourself for the times you pushed your needs aside for someone else. Offer yourself grace and a promise to do better.
10.
I am the only author of my life, and I choose to live authentically.
Imagine a day where you do absolutely nothing to please anyone else. What does your schedule look like, and how do you feel?
